annotator_steps: Find the relevant CDC guidance on continuing opioid therapy.
Identify the official CDC page containing the abrupt-discontinuation recommendation.
Open the CDC guidance page and verify the exact recommendation text.
Answer only after confirming the recommendation from the CDC source.
V1-R04.true_answer: No. CDC guidance says opioid therapy should not be discontinued abruptly when there are no indications of a life-threatening issue.
accepted_answers: No. CDC says opioid therapy should not be discontinued abruptly unless there is a life-threatening issue.
No. The CDC guidance says not to discontinue opioid therapy abruptly in the absence of a life-threatening issue.
